channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: FIFA U-turns on water bottle policy in US, Canada stadiums after backlash
item.description: New York Mayor Mamdani was among those critical of FIFA’s decision to ban water bottles at World Cup stadiums.

item.title: Giant crowds greet Pope Leo at public mass in Madrid, Spain
item.description: The pontiff praises Madrid as a beacon of inclusion as about 1.2 million people gather for Sunday mass.

item.title: Los Angeles tiny homes move people off streets amid World Cup spotlight
item.description: Thousands of people in Los Angeles are living in city-backed tiny homes as part of efforts to reduce homelessness.

item.title: Armenians go to polls in test of PM’s pivot to Europe amid Russian pressure
item.description: Two political blocs and 17 parties taking part in the election after campaign shaped by security and identity issues.

item.title: One killed, five wounded in shooting attack in Israel: Medics
item.description: Israeli police say the suspect is a Palestinian with Israeli citizenship.

item.title: 1 killed and 5 injured in Israel shootings
item.description: Multiple shooting attacks in Israel have killed 1 person and injured five others, Israeli police say.

item.title: Israeli settlers threaten to kill Palestinian farmer and harass sheep
item.description: A Palestinian farmer recounts the moment a large group of Israeli settlers hit him and harassed his livestock.

item.title: An Eid celebration on a Gaza rooftop turns into a ‘horror movie’
item.description: Israel's continued attacks on homes in Gaza are contributing to the ongoing genocide in the enclave.

item.title: Kosovo votes again amid political deadlock, seeking EU and NATO progress
item.description: Deadlock hampers Kosovo’s EU ambitions as voters head to polls amid economic struggles and political discord.

item.title: How have countries around the world responded to the US-Israel war on Iran?
item.description: The war, which has entered its 100th day, has triggered global energy crisis, drawing concerns from world leaders.

item.title: Hundred days of the US-Israel war on Iran
item.description: As the war on Iran marks 100 days, a complete truce still seems elusive, with the loss of life mounting in the region.

item.title: How the hosts are preparing for an Ebola outbreak during World Cup 2026
item.description: The US, Mexico and Canada have announced Ebola-related travel restrictions before the start of the World Cup.

item.title: Iran criticizes US refusal to grant visas for World Cup delegation
item.description: Iran’s ambassador to Mexico expressed concern over the reported denial of US visas to the World Cup delegation.

item.title: Who are the best star players to watch at World Cup 2026?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup will bring together football stars from across the globe, ranging from legends to emerging talents.

item.title: Massive human wave in Mexico ahead of World Cup 2026
item.description: Massive human wave in Mexico ahead of World Cup 2026

item.title: The accomplishments of 100 days of war on Iran are undeniable
item.description: The US has not just decimated Iran's nuclear programme and military, it has also degraded the power of its regime.

item.title: Iran after 100 days of war: The triumph of survival
item.description: Tehran sees its ability to preserve its governing system amid a regime-change war as a clear victory.

item.title: Pakistan’s Naqvi visits Iran with ‘special letter’ for supreme leader
item.description: Mediator Pakistan ramps up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran war as Gulf countries warn of escalation.

item.title: At least a dozen injured from shooting attack near Ohio festival
item.description: At least 12 people were wounded, two critically, in a shooting near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio.

item.title: Lebanon army chief in Pakistan, funeral plans for soldiers killed by Israel
item.description: Lebanon's army chief Rodolphe Haykal left for Pakistan in a surprise visit as relentless fighting continues.

item.title: Messi sits out Argentina’s pre-World Cup friendly due to hamstring strain
item.description: Messi's recovery depends on his 'clinical and functional progress', Argentina officials say before title defence.

item.title: Iran war day 100: US, Iran trade attacks again, raising tensions
item.description: Tensions rise as US shoots down Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz, vital for international maritime traffic.

item.title: Teenager Antonelli snatches Monaco pole with ‘magic lap’ for Mercedes
item.description: F1 championship leader becomes the first Italian since Jarno Trulli in 2004 to take pole position in the principality.

item.title: Outrage over Palestinian ‘dog rape’ joke at Tribeca Film Festival
item.description: A US influencer and actor have caused outrage after mocking Palestinians subject to rape in Israeli prisons.

item.title: Why are players being issued visas just days before the World Cup?
item.description: Last-minute visas are being issued for players competing in the FIFA World Cup. Al Jazeera's Mohammad Saleh explains.
